message:
fix(cards): KubeKong barrels never spawned due to unstable callbacks (#6304) (#6311)

@xonas1101 reported "no barrels been thrown by kong". Root cause
verified in KubeKong.tsx:

`getOnLadder` (line 126) and `checkPlatformCollision` (line 139)
were plain function declarations inside the component body, so both
got a fresh reference on every render. The game-loop useEffect at
line 632 has them in its dep array:

    }, [isPlaying, gameOver, getOnLadder, checkPlatformCollision,
        level, lives])

That means every render of the component re-ran the effect, which
cleared the old setInterval, started a new one, and reset the
effect-local `barrelSpawnCounter = 0`. The game loop ticks at 33ms
and the spawn threshold is 40-120 ticks (≈1.3-4s), but React
re-renders happen much more often than that — so the counter
never reached the threshold and Kong never threw a single barrel.

Fix: wrap both helpers in `useCallback` with an empty dep array.
They only read module-level constants (`PLAYER_WIDTH`,
`PLAYER_HEIGHT`, `LADDERS`, `PLATFORMS`, `getPlatformY`) so [] is
safe. The effect's dep array is unchanged; the refs it sees are
now stable and the setInterval runs uninterrupted.

Closes #6304

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
